headline: Baker Hughes (BKR) Could Be 20% Below Fair Value On Chart Industries Deal
summary: Baker Hughes (BKR) has just closed its acquisition of Chart Industries and appointed veteran executive Jim Apostolides to lead the new Chart segment, putting fresh attention on how this deal reshapes the stock’s risk profile. See our latest analysis for Baker Hughes. Baker Hughes shares have pulled back over the past month, with a 30 day share price return down 8.34%, yet the 1 year total shareholder return of 50.56% and 5 year total shareholder return of 218.96% point to strong longer term...

headline: Baker Hughes Completes Acquisition of Chart Industries
summary: Represents a major milestone in Baker Hughes’ ongoing portfolio management strategy to become a higher-value, leading industrialized energy solutions companyExpect $325 million in annualized cost synergies by year three after close; commercial synergy opportunities represent additional upsideChart Industries will be a third operating segment, reflecting the scale and strategic importance of its differentiated capabilities HOUSTON and LONDON, July 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Baker Hughes Company

headline: Baker Hughes (BKR) Stock Looks Undervalued As Its 219% Run Continues
summary: Baker Hughes stock has delivered a very strong 5 year return, yet its current valuation checks and an intrinsic value estimate using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach both suggest the shares may still be pricing in a discount to the underlying business. Over the past 5 years, Baker Hughes has returned 219%, which puts recent short term pullbacks into the context of a long period of strong shareholder gains. New contracts and acquisitions in liquefied natural gas and power for data...

headline: Barclays Maintains Equal-Weight on Baker Hughes, Lowers Price Target to $72
summary: Barclays  analyst David Anderson   maintains Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) with a Equal-Weight and lowers the price target from $74 to $72.
